summary: Joe Reis hosts a spontaneous live AMA session where he fields audience questions about the current and future state of data and AI. The conversation ranges from his philosophical views on industry dynamics (referencing his 'Givers and Takers' article) to practical advice on organizing data teams and real-world AI use cases.
key_points: Live unscripted discussion covering data and AI landscape
Explores philosophy behind 'Givers and Takers' industry dynamics
Practical advice on organizing data teams
money_quotes: The conversation covers a wide range of topics, from the philosophy behind my recent 'Givers and Takers' article to the practicalities of organizing data teams and the practical use cases for AI I'm seeing.
key_insight: Reis offers unfiltered, wide-ranging perspectives on how data teams should be organized and where AI is actually proving useful in practice.
